summary: Khailo describes building a peer-to-peer file transfer application on top of his Fire★ platform, using a pull-based chunked transfer protocol written in Lua. The post walks through the algorithm design, where receivers request chunks from senders, enabling natural multiplexing with other activities like chat and drawing.
key_points: File transfer app built on Fire★'s basic primitives rather than being hardcoded into the platform
Pull-based chunk transfer model enables natural rate limiting and multiplexing
Multiple simultaneous file transfers and multi-peer sends work seamlessly
All messages are encrypted and sent peer-to-peer with no central server
money_quotes: This pull model provides an obvious advantage, which is that it allows a limited rate of transfer allowing multiplexing with other messages that would be sent. So you can keep chatting, or drawing, or whatever while the file transferred.

Another obvious advantage to the pull model is that you can send multiple files at a time and they will be properly multiplexed. And if you have multiple people you are sending the file to, each one can receive it at their own speed.
This file transfer application is not built into Fire★, but is built on basic building blocks.
You can now chat, draw, and transfer files with multiple peers via p2p where all messages are encrypted and private.
key_insight: A pull-based chunked file transfer protocol naturally solves multiplexing and multi-peer rate control problems, and building it as a user-space app rather than a platform primitive demonstrates the extensibility of the Fire★ P2P framework.
